title: Accessibility Framework Skill ARIA Pattern
description: Validates per-pattern YAML items authored under .github/skills/accessibility/<framework-id>/items/<id>.yml for itemKind 'pattern'. The shape captures W3C ARIA Authoring Practices Guide composite-widget patterns: identifier, human-readable name, paraphrased summary, keyboard model taxonomy, focus-management strategy, related ARIA roles/states/properties, cross-walk to sibling Framework Skills (typically wcag-2-2 success criteria), and authoritative reference URLs. Identifiers are lower-kebab with hyphenated slugs matching the canonical APG pattern URL slug.

id.description: Lower-kebab pattern identifier matching the canonical W3C APG URL slug (for example 'combobox-autocomplete-list', 'dialog-modal', 'treeview-file-directory').

title.description: Human-readable pattern name as it appears in the W3C APG table of contents.

itemKind.description: Discriminator marking this item as an ARIA APG pattern.

summary.description: Paraphrased one-sentence description of the pattern (<=200 chars). Authors MUST NOT fabricate; paraphrase the canonical APG pattern page.

keyboardModel.description: Coarse-grained taxonomy labels describing the dominant keyboard interaction model (for example 'menu', 'listbox', 'grid', 'modal', 'inline', 'tabs', 'tree', 'spinbutton', 'slider', 'toolbar').

focusManagement.description: Focus-management strategy: 'trap' (focus is trapped within the widget, e.g. modal dialog), 'roving' (single tab stop with arrow-key navigation among descendants), 'single' (widget is a single tab stop with no descendant focus), 'none' (no special focus handling beyond browser defaults).

relatedRoles.description: ARIA role identifiers participating in the pattern (for example 'combobox', 'listbox', 'option').

requiredStates.description: ARIA state attributes the pattern requires authors to manage (for example 'aria-expanded', 'aria-selected', 'aria-checked').

requiredProperties.description: ARIA property attributes the pattern requires authors to declare (for example 'aria-controls', 'aria-haspopup', 'aria-labelledby').

relatedSuccessCriteria.description: Cross-walk identifiers to sibling Framework Skill items, formatted as '<framework>:<item-id>' (for example 'wcag-2-2:2-1-1', 'wcag-2-2:4-1-2').

referenceImplementationUrl.description: Canonical W3C APG pattern page URL containing the normative description and reference example.

references.description: Authoritative references (canonical APG pattern URL is required; additional links MAY include WAI-ARIA spec sections or technique pages).

evidenceHints.description: FSI v1.0 shared evidence-citation hints (relative glob paths) consumed by FSI-aware planners when the parent manifest declares 'evidenceFormat: shared'. Each token MUST satisfy EF-02 lexical sanity (no leading '/', no '..', no whitespace).
